3	// dispatch.go owns the SD-024 dispatch seam shared by execute-bead and the
4	// post-merge reviewer. It threads durable knobs from a sealed
5	// config.ResolvedConfig with per-invocation plumbing/intent from an
6	// AgentRunRuntime through the same service-or-runner routing used by
7	// RunWithConfigViaService for `ddx agent run`.
8	
9	import (
10		"context"
11		"fmt"
12	
13		agentlib "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent"
14		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
15	)
16	
17	// dispatchViaResolvedConfig is the internal SD-024 dispatch seam shared by
18	// execute-bead's worker and the post-merge reviewer. It resolves how the
19	// agent invocation is executed (test-injected runner, pre-built service, or
20	// a fresh service constructed from projectRoot) and routes through
21	// executeOnService so every durable knob lands on the dispatched request
22	// exactly once.
23	//
24	// Resolution order:
25	//  1. runner (test injection seam) — used directly via runner.Run after
26	//     applying any AgentRunRuntime overrides.
27	//  2. svc (pre-built service) — used via executeOnService.
28	//  3. Fallback: construct a fresh service via NewServiceFromWorkDir(projectRoot)
29	//     and dispatch via executeOnService.
30	//
31	// Override fields on runtime (HarnessOverride, ModelOverride,
32	// PermissionsOverride, SessionLogDirOverride) take precedence over the
33	// matching rcfg accessors when non-empty, so callers can pin one knob for
34	// a single invocation without re-resolving the full ResolvedConfig.
35	func dispatchViaResolvedConfig(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, svc agentlib.DdxAgent, runner AgentRunner, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime AgentRunRuntime) (*Result, error) {
36		if runner != nil {
37			return runner.Run(buildRunArgsFromConfig(ctx, rcfg, runtime))
38		}
39		if svc == nil {
40			built, err := NewServiceFromWorkDir(projectRoot)
41			if err != nil {
42				return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent: build service: %w", err)
43			}
44			svc = built
45		}
46		return executeOnService(ctx, svc, projectRoot, rcfg, runtime)
47	}
48	
49	// buildRunArgsFromConfig assembles a RunArgs value for the test
50	// injection runner path. It applies AgentRunRuntime overrides so a caller
51	// (execute-bead worker, post-merge reviewer) can pin one durable knob for
52	// a single invocation without re-resolving the full ResolvedConfig.
53	func buildRunArgsFromConfig(ctx context.Context, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime AgentRunRuntime) RunArgs {
54		harness := runtime.HarnessOverride
55		if harness == "" {
56			harness = rcfg.Harness()
57		}
58		model := runtime.ModelOverride
59		if model == "" {
60			model = rcfg.Model()
61		}
62		permissions := runtime.PermissionsOverride
63		if permissions == "" {
64			permissions = rcfg.Permissions()
65		}
66		sessionLogDir := runtime.SessionLogDirOverride
67		if sessionLogDir == "" {
68			sessionLogDir = rcfg.SessionLogDir()
69		}
70	
71		var opts RunArgs
72		opts.Context = ctx
73		opts.Harness = harness
74		// evidence:allow-unbounded reason="caller is responsible for bounding the prompt before invoking dispatchViaResolvedConfig; downstream executeOnService hits readPromptFileBounded for PromptFile inputs"
75		opts.Prompt = runtime.Prompt
76		opts.PromptFile = runtime.PromptFile
77		opts.PromptSource = runtime.PromptSource
78		opts.Correlation = runtime.Correlation
79		opts.Model = model
80		opts.Provider = rcfg.Provider()
81		opts.ModelRef = rcfg.ModelRef()
82		opts.Effort = rcfg.Effort()
83		opts.Timeout = rcfg.Timeout()
84		opts.WallClock = rcfg.WallClock()
85		opts.WorkDir = runtime.WorkDir
86		opts.Permissions = permissions
87		opts.SessionLogDir = sessionLogDir
88		return opts
89	}

18	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime carries the non-serializable plumbing and
19	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
20	// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, tier bounds, etc.) live on
21	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.
22	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
23		Log          io.Writer
24		EventSink    io.Writer
25		ProgressCh   chan<- ProgressEvent
26		PreClaimHook func(ctx context.Context) error
27		Once         bool

32		WorkerID     string

102	// SatisfactionChecker evaluates whether a bead that returned no_changes is
103	// already satisfied and should be closed, or is still unresolved and should
104	// receive retry suppression. noChangesCount is the cumulative count including
105	// the current attempt.
106	//
107	// When satisfied is true the caller closes the bead with the returned evidence
108	// string as the detail. When false the caller applies a retry cooldown and
109	// leaves the bead open.
110	type SatisfactionChecker interface {
111		CheckSatisfied(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount int) (satisfied bool, evidence string, err error)
112	}

116	
117	func (f SatisfactionCheckerFunc) CheckSatisfied(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount int) (bool, string, error) {
118		return f(ctx, beadID, noChangesCount)

189	// ExecuteBeadWorker drains the current single-project execution-ready queue.
190	// It intentionally does not retry a failed/conflicted bead again in the same
191	// process run; a later operator-driven invocation can create the next attempt.
192	type ExecuteBeadWorker struct {
193		Store               ExecuteBeadLoopStore
194		Executor            ExecuteBeadExecutor
195		SatisfactionChecker SatisfactionChecker // nil → count-based default
196		Now                 func() time.Time
197		// Reviewer, when non-nil, is called after every successful merge to
198		// validate the commit against the bead's acceptance criteria. When nil,
199		// post-merge review is skipped (same behaviour as --no-review).
200		Reviewer BeadReviewer
201	}

203	// emitProgress sends a ProgressEvent to runtime.ProgressCh non-blocking.
204	// If ch is nil or full the event is silently dropped.
205	func emitProgress(ch chan<- ProgressEvent, evt ProgressEvent) {
206		if ch == nil {
207			return
208		}
209		select {
210		case ch <- evt:
211		default:
212		}
213	}

215	// newProgressEvent builds a ProgressEvent with a random event_id and current timestamp.
216	func newProgressEvent(workerID, projectID, beadID, attemptID, harness, model, profile, phase string, phaseSeq int, heartbeat bool, elapsedMS int64) ProgressEvent {
217		return ProgressEvent{
218			EventID:   "evt-" + randomProgressID(),
219			AttemptID: attemptID,
220			WorkerID:  workerID,
221			ProjectID: projectID,
222			BeadID:    beadID,
223			Harness:   harness,
224			Model:     model,
225			Profile:   profile,
226			Phase:     phase,
227			PhaseSeq:  phaseSeq,
228			Heartbeat: heartbeat,
229			TS:        time.Now().UTC(),
230			ElapsedMS: elapsedMS,
231		}
232	}

234	func randomProgressID() string {

236	}

238	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) Run(ctx context.Context, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) (*ExecuteBeadLoopResult, error) {
239		if w.Store == nil {
240			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: store is required")
241		}
242		if w.Executor == nil {
243			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: executor is required")
244		}
245	
246		now := w.Now
247		if now == nil {
248			now = time.Now
249		}
250		assignee := rcfg.Assignee()
251		if assignee == "" {
252			assignee = "ddx"
253		}
254		noProgressCooldown := rcfg.NoProgressCooldown()
255		if noProgressCooldown <= 0 {
256			noProgressCooldown = 6 * time.Hour
257		}
258		maxNoChangesBeforeClose := rcfg.MaxNoChangesBeforeClose()
259		if maxNoChangesBeforeClose <= 0 {
260			maxNoChangesBeforeClose = 3
261		}
262		heartbeatInterval := rcfg.HeartbeatInterval()
263		if heartbeatInterval <= 0 {
264			heartbeatInterval = bead.HeartbeatInterval
265		}
266		harness := rcfg.Harness()
267		model := rcfg.Model()
268		profile := rcfg.Profile()
269	
270		result := &ExecuteBeadLoopResult{}
271		attempted := make(map[string]struct{})
272	
273		emit := func(eventType string, data map[string]any) {
274			writeLoopEvent(runtime.EventSink, runtime.SessionID, eventType, data, now().UTC())
275		}
276	
277		emit("loop.start", map[string]any{
278			"worker_id":    runtime.WorkerID,
279			"project_root": runtime.ProjectRoot,
280			"harness":      harness,
281			"model":        model,
282			"session_id":   runtime.SessionID,
283			"assignee":     assignee,
284			"once":         runtime.Once,
285		})
286		defer func() {
287			emit("loop.end", map[string]any{
288				"attempts":            result.Attempts,
289				"successes":           result.Successes,
290				"failures":            result.Failures,
291				"last_failure_status": result.LastFailureStatus,
292			})
293		}()
294	
295		for {
296			// Respect context cancellation between iterations. Without this check,
297			// a Stop() request (which cancels ctx) would only take effect during
298			// the idle poll sleep — the loop would happily claim the next ready
299			// bead as soon as the current Execute returned, ignoring the cancel.
300			if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
301				return result, err
302			}
303	
304			candidate, ok, err := w.nextCandidate(attempted, runtime.LabelFilter)
305			if err != nil {
306				return result, err
307			}
308			if !ok {
309				if result.Attempts == 0 {
310					result.NoReadyWork = true
311					if diag, ok := w.Store.(readyDiagnoser); ok {
312						if breakdown, bErr := diag.ReadyExecutionBreakdown(); bErr == nil {
313							result.NoReadyWorkDetail = NoReadyWorkBreakdown{
314								SkippedEpics:       breakdown.SkippedEpics,
315								SkippedOnCooldown:  breakdown.SkippedOnCooldown,
316								SkippedNotEligible: breakdown.SkippedNotEligible,
317								SkippedSuperseded:  breakdown.SkippedSuperseded,
318								NextRetryAfter:     breakdown.NextRetryAfter,
319							}
320						}
321					}
322				}
323				if runtime.PollInterval <= 0 {
324					return result, nil
325				}
326				if err := sleepWithContext(ctx, runtime.PollInterval); err != nil {
327					return result, err
328				}
329				continue
330			}
331	
332			attempted[candidate.ID] = struct{}{}
333	
334			// Pre-claim hook: fetch origin + verify ancestry before claiming.
335			// On error the bead is skipped for this iteration; the loop
336			// continues (ctx is not cancelled).
337			if runtime.PreClaimHook != nil {
338				if hookErr := runtime.PreClaimHook(ctx); hookErr != nil {
339					if runtime.Log != nil {
340						_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "pre-claim hook: %v (skipping %s)\n", hookErr, candidate.ID)
341					}
342					emit("preclaim.skipped", map[string]any{
343						"bead_id": candidate.ID,
344						"reason":  hookErr.Error(),
345					})
346					continue
347				}
348			}
349	
350			if err := w.Store.Claim(candidate.ID, assignee); err != nil {
351				continue
352			}
353	
354			emit("bead.claimed", map[string]any{
355				"bead_id":  candidate.ID,
356				"title":    candidate.Title,
357				"assignee": assignee,
358			})
359	
360			if runtime.Log != nil {
361				if candidate.Title != "" {
362					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "\n▶ %s: %s\n", candidate.ID, candidate.Title)
363				} else {
364					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "\n▶ %s\n", candidate.ID)
365				}
366			}
367	
368			// Generate a provisional attempt_id for progress events.
369			// The real attempt_id is assigned inside ExecuteBead; we use this
370			// for queueing/running events and replace with the real one once known.
371			provAttemptID := time.Now().UTC().Format("20060102T150405") + "-" + randomProgressID()
372			runStart := now()
373			phaseSeq := 0
374			nextPhase := func(phase string, heartbeat bool) {
375				phaseSeq++
376				emitProgress(runtime.ProgressCh, newProgressEvent(
377					runtime.WorkerID, runtime.ProjectRoot, candidate.ID, provAttemptID,
378					harness, model, profile,
379					phase, phaseSeq, heartbeat, now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(),
380				))
381			}
382	
383			nextPhase("queueing", false)
384	
385			hbCtx, hbCancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
386			var hbWG sync.WaitGroup
387			hbWG.Add(1)
388			go func(beadID string) {
389				defer hbWG.Done()
390				ticker := time.NewTicker(heartbeatInterval)
391				defer ticker.Stop()
392				for {
393					select {
394					case <-hbCtx.Done():
395						return
396					case <-ticker.C:
397						_ = w.Store.Heartbeat(beadID)
398					}
399				}
400			}(candidate.ID)
401	
402			nextPhase("running", false)
403	
404			report, err := w.Executor.Execute(ctx, candidate.ID)
405			hbCancel()
406			hbWG.Wait()
407			if err != nil {
408				report = ExecuteBeadReport{
409					BeadID: candidate.ID,
410					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
411					Detail: err.Error(),
412				}
413			}
414			if report.BeadID == "" {
415				report.BeadID = candidate.ID
416			}
417			if report.Status == "" {
418				report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
419			}
420			if report.Detail == "" {
421				report.Detail = ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(report.Status, "", "")
422			}
423	
424			result.Attempts++
425	
426			if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess {
427				appendLoopRoutingEvidence(w.Store, candidate.ID, report, now().UTC())
428				// Close the bead early when review is skipped. The closure gate
429				// (ddx-e30e60a9) accepts this path because closing_commit_sha is
430				// set and there is no malformed-APPROVE event to reject.
431				reviewApproved := true
432				reviewSkipped := w.Reviewer == nil || runtime.NoReview || HasBeadLabel(candidate.Labels, "review:skip")
433	
434				if reviewSkipped {
435					if err := w.Store.CloseWithEvidence(candidate.ID, report.SessionID, report.ResultRev); err != nil {
436						return result, err
437					}
438				}
439	
440				if w.Reviewer != nil && !runtime.NoReview && !HasBeadLabel(candidate.Labels, "review:skip") {
441					reviewRes, reviewErr := w.Reviewer.ReviewBead(ctx, candidate.ID, report.ResultRev, report.Harness, report.Model)
442					if reviewErr != nil {
443						// FEAT-022 §12+§14: classify the failure into the four-class
444						// taxonomy and count prior review-error events scoped to the
445						// current result_rev. On the Nth failure emit a terminal
446						// review-manual-required event instead of yet another
447						// review-error, parking the bead so a subsequent loop
448						// iteration does NOT re-execute primary.
449						class := classifyReviewError(reviewErr, reviewRes)
450						prior := countPriorReviewErrors(w.Store, candidate.ID, report.ResultRev)
451						attemptCount := prior + 1
452						maxRetries := rcfg.ReviewMaxRetries()
453						if maxRetries <= 0 {
454							maxRetries = DefaultReviewMaxRetries
455						}
456						errSummary := EventBodySummary{
457							InputBytes:  0,
458							OutputBytes: 0,
459						}
460						if reviewRes != nil {
461							errSummary = EventBodySummary{
462								Harness:     reviewRes.ReviewerHarness,
463								Model:       reviewRes.ReviewerModel,
464								InputBytes:  reviewRes.InputBytes,
465								OutputBytes: reviewRes.OutputBytes,
466								ElapsedMS:   reviewRes.DurationMS,
467							}
468						}
469						if attemptCount >= maxRetries {
470							body := appendEventSummary(reviewErrorEventBody(class, attemptCount, report.ResultRev, reviewErr.Error()), errSummary)
471							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
472								Kind:      "review-manual-required",
473								Summary:   class,
474								Body:      body,
475								Actor:     assignee,
476								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
477								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
478							})
479							// Park the bead with a long cooldown so subsequent
480							// loop iterations do NOT re-pick it for primary
481							// re-execution. Reviewer-failure invariant (§13) is
482							// preserved: the bead is NOT closed.
483							parkUntil := now().UTC().Add(365 * 24 * time.Hour)
484							_ = w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, parkUntil, "review-manual-required", class)
485						} else {
486							body := appendEventSummary(reviewErrorEventBody(class, attemptCount, report.ResultRev, reviewErr.Error()), errSummary)
487							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
488								Kind:      "review-error",
489								Summary:   class,
490								Body:      body,
491								Actor:     assignee,
492								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
493								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
494							})
495						}
496					} else {
497						report.ReviewVerdict = string(reviewRes.Verdict)
498						report.ReviewRationale = reviewRes.Rationale
499						// Persist the full reviewer stream as an artifact so the
500						// event body never carries the raw stream (ddx-f8a11202).
501						// On error the artifact path is empty and the event body
502						// still contains the short verdict summary; callers of this
503						// loop can recover the full text from the reviewer session
504						// log if the artifact write failed.
505						artifactPath, artifactErr := persistReviewerStream(runtime.ProjectRoot, candidate.ID, report.AttemptID, reviewRes.RawOutput)
506						if artifactErr != nil && runtime.Log != nil {
507							_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "reviewer stream artifact: %v\n", artifactErr)
508						}
509	
510						reviewSummary := EventBodySummary{
511							Harness:     reviewRes.ReviewerHarness,
512							Model:       reviewRes.ReviewerModel,
513							InputBytes:  reviewRes.InputBytes,
514							OutputBytes: reviewRes.OutputBytes,
515							ElapsedMS:   reviewRes.DurationMS,
516						}
517						switch reviewRes.Verdict {
518						case VerdictApprove:
519							// Approved: record the verdict event and then close.
520							// Closure must land AFTER the review event so the
521							// gate (ddx-e30e60a9) sees the terminal verdict.
522							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
523								Kind:      "review",
524								Summary:   "APPROVE",
525								Body:      appendEventSummary(reviewEventBody("APPROVE", reviewRes.Rationale, artifactPath), reviewSummary),
526								Actor:     assignee,
527								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
528								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
529							})
530							if cerr := w.Store.CloseWithEvidence(candidate.ID, report.SessionID, report.ResultRev); cerr != nil {
531								return result, cerr
532							}
533						case VerdictRequestChanges:
534							// Needs fixes: record the review verdict, then reopen
535							// with findings in notes. The review event must land
536							// even on non-approve paths so review-outcomes can
537							// attribute the rejection to the originating tier.
538							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
539								Kind:      "review",
540								Summary:   "REQUEST_CHANGES",
541								Body:      appendEventSummary(reviewEventBody("REQUEST_CHANGES", reviewRes.Rationale, artifactPath), reviewSummary),
542								Actor:     assignee,
543								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
544								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
545							})
546							reopenNotes := reviewRes.Rationale
547							if reopenNotes == "" {
548								reopenNotes = reviewRes.RawOutput
549							}
550							if reopenErr := w.Store.Reopen(candidate.ID, "review: REQUEST_CHANGES", reopenNotes); reopenErr != nil {
551								return result, reopenErr
552							}
553							report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestChanges
554							report.Detail = "post-merge review: REQUEST_CHANGES"
555							reviewApproved = false
556						case VerdictBlock:
557							rationale := strings.TrimSpace(reviewRes.Rationale)
558							if rationale == "" {
559								_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
560									Kind:      "review-malfunction",
561									Summary:   "BLOCK without rationale",
562									Body:      appendEventSummary(reviewEventBody("BLOCK without rationale", "", artifactPath), reviewSummary),
563									Actor:     assignee,
564									Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
565									CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
566								})
567								report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction
568								report.Detail = "post-merge review: malformed BLOCK verdict (missing rationale)"
569								report.ReviewRationale = ""
570								reviewApproved = false
571								break
572							}
573							// Cannot proceed: record the verdict, then reopen and
574							// flag for human with BLOCK marker plus actionable rationale.
575							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
576								Kind:      "review",
577								Summary:   "BLOCK",
578								Body:      appendEventSummary(rationale, reviewSummary),
579								Actor:     assignee,
580								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
581								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
582							})
583							blockNotes := "REVIEW:BLOCK\n\n" + rationale
584							if reopenErr := w.Store.Reopen(candidate.ID, "review: BLOCK", blockNotes); reopenErr != nil {
585								return result, reopenErr
586							}
587							report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewBlock
588							report.Detail = "post-merge review: BLOCK (flagged for human)"
589							reviewApproved = false
590						}
591					}
592				}
593	
594				if reviewApproved {
595					result.Successes++
596					result.LastSuccessAt = now().UTC()
597				} else {
598					result.Failures++
599					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
600				}
601			} else {
602				if err := w.Store.Unclaim(candidate.ID); err != nil {
603					return result, err
604				}
605				if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges {
606					count, cerr := w.Store.IncrNoChangesCount(candidate.ID)
607					if cerr != nil {
608						return result, cerr
609					}
610					satisfied, evidence, aerr := w.adjudicateNoChanges(ctx, candidate.ID, count, maxNoChangesBeforeClose, report.NoChangesRationale, candidate.Acceptance, runtime.ProjectRoot)
611					if aerr != nil {
612						return result, aerr
613					}
614					if satisfied {
615						// Adjudication confirmed bead is already satisfied.
616						// Set the terminal status BEFORE the close so the late
617						// executeBeadLoopEvent append captures "already_satisfied"
618						// (not "no_changes"), and emit an early execute-bead
619						// evidence event so the closure gate accepts even when
620						// BaseRev is empty (test fixtures and genuinely-no-commit
621						// satisfied beads).
622						report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied
623						if evidence != "" {
624							// Checker evidence explains why the bead is being closed;
625							// it takes precedence over the executor's attempt detail.
626							report.Detail = evidence
627						}
628						_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, executeBeadLoopEvent(report, assignee, now().UTC()))
629						if cerr := w.Store.CloseWithEvidence(candidate.ID, report.SessionID, report.BaseRev); cerr != nil {
630							return result, cerr
631						}
632						result.Successes++
633						result.LastSuccessAt = now().UTC()
634					} else {
635						// Unresolved: suppress immediate retry so the queue can
636						// move on to other beads.
637						if shouldSuppressNoProgress(report) {
638							retryAfter := now().UTC().Add(noProgressCooldown)
639							if cerr := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, retryAfter, report.Status, report.Detail); cerr != nil {
640								return result, cerr
641							}
642							report.RetryAfter = retryAfter.Format(time.RFC3339)
643						}
644						result.Failures++
645						result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
646					}
647				} else if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition {
648					// Executor declined the bead because it is too large to
649					// deliver in one pass. Record a structured
650					// `decomposition-recommendation` event and park the bead
651					// long enough that subsequent loop iterations do NOT
652					// re-attempt it. An operator (or helix-evolve) clears the
653					// cooldown — typically by splitting the bead.
654					body, mErr := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
655						"rationale":            report.DecompositionRationale,
656						"recommended_subbeads": report.DecompositionRecommendation,
657						"detail":               report.Detail,
658						"base_rev":             report.BaseRev,
659						"session_id":           report.SessionID,
660					})
661					bodyStr := ""
662					if mErr == nil {
663						bodyStr = string(body)
664					} else {
665						bodyStr = fmt.Sprintf("rationale=%s\nrecommended_subbeads=%v",
666							report.DecompositionRationale, report.DecompositionRecommendation)
667					}
668					summary := "agent declined: needs decomposition"
669					if n := len(report.DecompositionRecommendation); n > 0 {
670						summary = fmt.Sprintf("%s (%d sub-beads)", summary, n)
671					}
672					_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
673						Kind:      "decomposition-recommendation",
674						Summary:   summary,
675						Body:      bodyStr,
676						Actor:     assignee,
677						Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
678						CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
679					})
680					parkUntil := now().UTC().Add(365 * 24 * time.Hour)
681					if err := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, parkUntil, report.Status, report.Detail); err != nil {
682						return result, err
683					}
684					report.RetryAfter = parkUntil.Format(time.RFC3339)
685					result.Failures++
686					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
687				} else if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed {
688					// Push failed after a successful local merge/ff. The bead is
689					// NOT closed (commits live only locally) — park it long enough
690					// that subsequent loop iterations refuse to re-pick it until
691					// an operator clears the state. The push stderr in
692					// report.Detail is recorded as last_detail so it surfaces on
693					// the bead and on any direct claim attempt.
694					parkUntil := now().UTC().Add(365 * 24 * time.Hour)
695					if err := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, parkUntil, report.Status, report.Detail); err != nil {
696						return result, err
697					}
698					report.RetryAfter = parkUntil.Format(time.RFC3339)
699					result.Failures++
700					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
701				} else {
702					if shouldSuppressNoProgress(report) {
703						retryAfter := now().UTC().Add(noProgressCooldown)
704						if err := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, retryAfter, report.Status, report.Detail); err != nil {
705							return result, err
706						}
707						report.RetryAfter = retryAfter.Format(time.RFC3339)
708					}
709					result.Failures++
710					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
711				}
712			}
713	
714			result.Results = append(result.Results, report)
715	
716			// Skip the late execute-bead append for already-satisfied beads —
717			// the satisfied path appends its own terminal event before
718			// CloseWithEvidence so the closure gate sees execution evidence.
719			// Duplicating it here would yield two identical events.
720			if report.Status != ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied {
721				if err := w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, executeBeadLoopEvent(report, assignee, now().UTC())); err != nil {
722					return result, err
723				}
724			}
725	
726			// Emit terminal progress phase event.
727			terminalPhase := "failed"
728			if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess || report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied {
729				terminalPhase = "done"
730			} else if report.PreserveRef != "" {
731				terminalPhase = "preserved"
732			}
733			// Use the real attempt_id from the report if available.
734			finalAttemptID := report.AttemptID
735			if finalAttemptID == "" {
736				finalAttemptID = provAttemptID
737			}
738			phaseSeq++
739			emitProgress(runtime.ProgressCh, ProgressEvent{
740				EventID:   "evt-" + randomProgressID(),
741				AttemptID: finalAttemptID,
742				WorkerID:  runtime.WorkerID,
743				ProjectID: runtime.ProjectRoot,
744				BeadID:    candidate.ID,
745				Harness:   harness,
746				Model:     model,
747				Profile:   profile,
748				Phase:     terminalPhase,
749				PhaseSeq:  phaseSeq,
750				Heartbeat: false,
751				TS:        now().UTC(),
752				ElapsedMS: now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(),
753				Message:   report.Detail,
754			})
755	
756			emit("bead.result", map[string]any{
757				"bead_id":              candidate.ID,
758				"status":               report.Status,
759				"detail":               report.Detail,
760				"session_id":           report.SessionID,
761				"result_rev":           report.ResultRev,
762				"base_rev":             report.BaseRev,
763				"preserve_ref":         report.PreserveRef,
764				"no_changes_rationale": report.NoChangesRationale,
765				"duration_ms":          now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(),
766			})
767	
768			if runtime.Log != nil {
769				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "✓ %s → %s\n", candidate.ID, formatLoopResult(report))
770			}
771	
772			if runtime.Once {
773				return result, nil
774			}
775		}
776	}

778	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) nextCandidate(attempted map[string]struct{}, labelFilter string) (bead.Bead, bool, error) {
779		ready, err := w.Store.ReadyExecution()
780		if err != nil {
781			return bead.Bead{}, false, err
782		}
783		for _, candidate := range ready {
784			if _, seen := attempted[candidate.ID]; seen {
785				continue
786			}
787			if labelFilter != "" && !HasBeadLabel(candidate.Labels, labelFilter) {
788				continue
789			}
790			return candidate, true, nil
791		}
792		return bead.Bead{}, false, nil
793	}
794	
795	// appendLoopRoutingEvidence records a kind:routing evidence event on the bead
796	// from the executor's ExecuteBeadReport, so that review-outcomes analytics can
797	// attribute a subsequent review verdict to the originating provider/model tier.
798	// Best-effort: errors and missing-provider cases are silently ignored.
799	func appendLoopRoutingEvidence(store BeadEventAppender, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport, createdAt time.Time) {
800		if store == nil || beadID == "" {
801			return
802		}
803		provider := report.Provider
804		if provider == "" {
805			provider = report.Harness
806		}
807		if provider == "" {
808			return
809		}
810		body, err := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
811			"resolved_provider": provider,
812			"resolved_model":    report.Model,
813			"fallback_chain":    []string{},
814			"requested_profile": report.RequestedProfile,
815			"requested_tier":    report.RequestedTier,
816			"resolved_tier":     report.ResolvedTier,
817			"escalation_count":  report.EscalationCount,
818			"final_tier":        report.FinalTier,
819		})
820		if err != nil {
821			return
822		}
823		summary := "provider=" + provider
824		if report.Model != "" {
825			summary += " model=" + report.Model
826		}
827		_ = store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
828			Kind:      "routing",
829			Summary:   summary,
830			Body:      string(body),
831			Actor:     "ddx",
832			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
833			CreatedAt: createdAt,
834		})
835	}

837	func executeBeadLoopEvent(report ExecuteBeadReport, actor string, createdAt time.Time) bead.BeadEvent {
838		parts := []string{}
839		if report.Detail != "" {
840			parts = append(parts, report.Detail)
841		}
842		if report.Tier != "" {
843			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("tier=%s", report.Tier))
844		}
845		if report.ProbeResult != "" {
846			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("probe_result=%s", report.ProbeResult))
847		}
848		if report.NoChangesRationale != "" {
849			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("rationale: %s", report.NoChangesRationale))
850		}
851		if report.ReviewRationale != "" {
852			parts = append(parts, report.ReviewRationale)
853		}
854		if report.PreserveRef != "" {
855			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("preserve_ref=%s", report.PreserveRef))
856		}
857		if report.ResultRev != "" {
858			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("result_rev=%s", report.ResultRev))
859		}
860		if report.BaseRev != "" {
861			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("base_rev=%s", report.BaseRev))
862		}
863		if report.RetryAfter != "" {
864			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("retry_after=%s", report.RetryAfter))
865		}
866	
867		return bead.BeadEvent{
868			Kind:      "execute-bead",
869			Summary:   report.Status,
870			Body:      strings.Join(parts, "\n"),
871			Actor:     actor,
872			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
873			CreatedAt: createdAt,
874		}
875	}

877	// writeLoopEvent emits one structured JSONL line to sink describing a
878	// milestone in an execute-bead loop run. Entries use the same envelope as
879	// the ddx-agent harness (session_id/seq/type/ts/data) so existing log
880	// aggregators (FormatSessionLogLines, ddx server workers log) can parse
881	// the stream uniformly. Errors are swallowed: structured logging must
882	// never break the core execute-loop.
883	func writeLoopEvent(sink io.Writer, sessionID, eventType string, data map[string]any, ts time.Time) {
884		if sink == nil {
885			return
886		}
887		if data == nil {
888			data = map[string]any{}
889		}
890		entry := map[string]any{
891			"session_id": sessionID,
892			"type":       eventType,
893			"ts":         ts.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339Nano),
894			"data":       data,
895		}
896		line, err := json.Marshal(entry)
897		if err != nil {
898			return
899		}
900		_, _ = sink.Write(line)
901		_, _ = sink.Write([]byte("\n"))
902	}

904	// formatLoopResult returns a concise human-readable summary of a bead execution
905	// result using merged/preserved/error terminology instead of raw status codes.
906	func formatLoopResult(report ExecuteBeadReport) string {
907		switch report.Status {
908		case ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess:
909			shortRev := report.ResultRev
910			if len(shortRev) > 8 {
911				shortRev = shortRev[:8]
912			}
913			if shortRev != "" {
914				return fmt.Sprintf("merged (%s)", shortRev)
915			}
916			return "merged"
917		case ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied:
918			return "already_satisfied"
919		case ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges:
920			if report.NoChangesRationale != "" {
921				return fmt.Sprintf("no_changes: %s", report.NoChangesRationale)
922			}
923			return "no_changes"
924		default:
925			detail := report.Detail
926			if detail == "" {
927				detail = report.Status
928			}
929			if report.PreserveRef != "" {
930				return fmt.Sprintf("preserved: %s", detail)
931			}
932			return fmt.Sprintf("error: %s", detail)
933		}
934	}
935	
936	// reCommitSHA matches a 7-to-40 character lowercase hex string that looks like a
937	// git commit SHA. Used to detect whether a no_changes rationale cites a prior commit.
938	var reCommitSHA = regexp.MustCompile(`\b[0-9a-f]{7,40}\b`)
939	
940	// reTestFuncName matches a Go test function name (TestXxx or BenchmarkXxx).
941	var reTestFuncName = regexp.MustCompile(`\b(?:Test|Benchmark)[A-Z]\w*\b`)
942	
943	// rationaleIsSpecific returns true when the rationale string contains a reference
944	// specific enough to treat a no_changes outcome as already_satisfied on the first
945	// attempt. Currently this means: the rationale cites a commit SHA (7+ hex chars)
946	// or a Go test function name. Vague rationales ("nothing to do") return false.
947	func rationaleIsSpecific(rationale string) bool {
948		if rationale == "" {
949			return false
950		}
951		return reCommitSHA.MatchString(rationale) || reTestFuncName.MatchString(rationale)
952	}
953	
954	// adjudicateNoChanges runs the no-change adjudication step for a bead.
955	// It returns (satisfied, evidence, err). When satisfied is true the bead
956	// should be closed as already_satisfied with the evidence string. When false
957	// retry suppression (cooldown) should be applied and the bead left open.
958	//
959	// If a SatisfactionChecker is configured it is called first. Otherwise:
960	//   - When the report carries a specific rationale (cites a commit SHA or test
961	//     name), the bead is closed as already_satisfied on the first occurrence.
962	//   - Otherwise the default count-based rule applies (close after maxNoChangesBeforeClose).
963	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) adjudicateNoChanges(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount, maxNoChangesBeforeClose int, rationale, acceptance, projectRoot string) (bool, string, error) {

966		}
967		candidate := false
968		evidence := ""
969		switch {
970		case rationaleIsSpecific(rationale):
971			candidate = true
972			evidence = rationale
973		case noChangesCount >= maxNoChangesBeforeClose:
974			candidate = true
975			evidence = fmt.Sprintf("no_changes on %d consecutive attempt(s); bead treated as already satisfied", noChangesCount)
976		}
977		if !candidate {
978			return false, "", nil
979		}
980		// Tighten the gate: when AC names structural properties (test functions,
981		// deleted files, removed struct fields), refuse already_satisfied unless
982		// each property holds in the worktree / rationale. Prevents false closes
983		// where a regression suite passes but the AC's specific contract is unmet.
984		if claims := ParseACClaims(acceptance); len(claims) > 0 {
985			if ok, why := VerifyACClaims(claims, projectRoot, rationale); !ok {
986				return false, why, nil

988		}
989		return true, evidence, nil

992	func shouldSuppressNoProgress(report ExecuteBeadReport) bool {
993		if report.BaseRev == "" || report.ResultRev == "" {
994			return false
995		}
996		return report.BaseRev == report.ResultRev
997	}

999	// classifyReviewError maps a reviewer error and partial result into one of the
1000	// four FEAT-022 §12 taxonomy classes. Resolution order:
1001	//  1. reviewRes.Error if it's already one of the canonical class identifiers
1002	//     (the reviewer set it explicitly — preferred path).
1003	//  2. The error string text (for backwards-compatible reviewers that only
1004	//     embed the class in their message).
1005	//  3. ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable as a fallback when the strict-parse error
1006	//     leaks through a reviewer that did not set Error.
1007	//  4. Default to transport (network/provider-side failure) when nothing else
1008	//     matches.
1009	func classifyReviewError(reviewErr error, reviewRes *ReviewResult) string {
1010		if reviewRes != nil {
1011			switch reviewRes.Error {
1012			case evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
1013				evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty,
1014				evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable,
1015				evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport:
1016				return reviewRes.Error
1017			}
1018		}
1019		msg := ""
1020		if reviewErr != nil {
1021			msg = reviewErr.Error()
1022		}
1023		switch {
1024		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow):
1025			return evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow
1026		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty):
1027			return evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty
1028		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable):
1029			return evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable
1030		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport):
1031			return evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport
1032		case reviewErr != nil && strings.Contains(msg, ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable.Error()):
1033			return evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable
1034		}
1035		return evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport
1036	}
1037	
1038	// reResultRevField extracts the result_rev value from a structured review-error
1039	// event body (one `result_rev=<sha>` line per event, written by
1040	// reviewErrorEventBody). The format is intentionally line-oriented so it
1041	// survives the AppendEvent body cap without losing the rev association.
1042	var reResultRevField = regexp.MustCompile(`(?m)^result_rev=([^\s]+)\s*$`)
1043	
1044	// countPriorReviewErrors returns the number of `review-error` events already
1045	// recorded against this bead whose body cites the given result_rev. This is
1046	// the FEAT-022 §14 retry counter — it is event-scoped (no separate counter
1047	// field on the bead) so a fresh result_rev naturally resets the count.
1048	func countPriorReviewErrors(store ExecuteBeadLoopStore, beadID, resultRev string) int {
1049		if resultRev == "" {
1050			return 0
1051		}
1052		events, err := store.Events(beadID)
1053		if err != nil {
1054			return 0
1055		}
1056		n := 0
1057		for _, ev := range events {
1058			if ev.Kind != "review-error" {
1059				continue
1060			}
1061			m := reResultRevField.FindStringSubmatch(ev.Body)
1062			if m == nil {
1063				continue
1064			}
1065			if m[1] == resultRev {
1066				n++
1067			}
1068		}
1069		return n
1070	}
1071	
1072	// reviewErrorEventBody is the canonical body shape for review-error and
1073	// review-manual-required events. It carries the failure class, attempt count,
1074	// and result_rev as discrete lines so operators can grep without parsing the
1075	// full reviewer error text. The trailing free-form message is the raw
1076	// reviewer-error string for forensics.
1077	func reviewErrorEventBody(class string, attemptCount int, resultRev, message string) string {
1078		var b strings.Builder
1079		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "failure_class=%s\n", class)
1080		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "attempt_count=%d\n", attemptCount)
1081		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "result_rev=%s\n", resultRev)
1082		if message != "" {
1083			b.WriteString("\n")
1084			b.WriteString(message)
1085		}
1086		return b.String()
1087	}

1092		}

1094		defer timer.Stop()
1095		select {
1096		case <-ctx.Done():
1097			return ctx.Err()
1098		case <-timer.C:
1099			return nil

Adopt ddx-agent routing point release: zero-interaction defaults, usable profiles, sharp overrides (DDx-side consumer)
---DESC---
## Status after codex review (2026-04-22)

Original plan was consolidated in this bead but **reduced in scope** after codex's review surfaced that most of the compatibility and data-model work belongs upstream in ddx-agent. This bead is now the **DDx-side tracker only**. Upstream work is queued as separate beads in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/` (prefix `agent-*`) and will land as a single tagged point release; nothing in DDx lands until that release is pulled.

## Goals (unchanged — user stated 2026-04-22)

**G1. Zero-interaction defaults.** `ddx agent run` and `ddx agent execute-bead` just work with no flags.

**G2. Usable profiles.** `--profile standard`, `--profile local` force clear, documented rules.

**G3. Usable overrides.** `--harness claude` forces claude. `--model opus-4.7` forces opus-4.7. Within requirements: available, quota OK, lowest cost.

## Upstream dependencies (tracked in ~/Projects/agent/.ddx/)

This bead blocks on a single upstream point release that carries:

- Profile catalog with first-class `default`, `local`, `standard` (and any others DDx needs to expose) + documented provider-preference rules.
- Supported-models allow-list on routing-inputs `HarnessEntry`; gating rejects non-allowed models even when `ExactPinSupport: true`.
- Typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` surfaced from `Execute` so explicit `--harness X --model Y` conflicts fail fast with actionable detail.
- Public `RouteDecision.Candidates` + typed error for "no viable candidate" with per-candidate rejection reasons — DDx's decision-trace consumers need this.
- Cost dimension on `ProviderEntry` (`CostUSDPer1kTokens`) + scoring tiebreak that prefers lower cost, with unknown-cost fallback, subscription-quota pricing, and near-quota demotion.
- Documentation for the profile catalog, override precedence, and "best provider" contract.

Once that release is tagged, DDx work below proceeds.

## DDx-side changes (reduced scope)

**D1. Dispatch sends a real spec.**

`workerDispatchAdapter.DispatchWorker` treats empty input as `{Profile: "default"}` explicitly. Dashboard Drain button calls the same path. No DDx-side default synthesis beyond "profile=default if unset."

**D2. Stop synthesizing a tier ladder by default.**

Default `ddx agent run` / `ddx agent execute-bead` / `ddx work` path calls `agentlib.ResolveRoute(Profile: spec.Profile)` with no Model string unless `spec.Model` was explicitly given. No per-tier iteration, no `ResolveTierModelRef` lookup, no preference-order fallback. The tier-ladder machinery becomes opt-in via `--escalate` only.

**D3. Preflight gate (defensive only, post-upstream).**

After `ResolveRoute` returns, DDx verifies the decision's `(Harness, Model)` is not in the typed-incompatibility set surfaced by upstream. The compatibility data comes from upstream — DDx does **not** duplicate the allow-list. Insertion point: `execute_bead_loop.go:352`, **before** `Claim`. Route/preflight failure exits the loop with a worker-level failure record; no bead is claimed, no burn cycle.

**D4. Deprecate DDx-side routing config.**

Deliberate breaking change in the same release that pulls the upstream point release:
- `agent.routing.default_harness` — field deleted; upstream's profile model makes it redundant.
- `agent.routing.profile_ladders` — kept but gated behind explicit `--escalate` only. Not read on the default path. Warning at config-load if set without `--escalate` ever being used in the life of the process.
- `agent.routing.model_overrides` — same treatment as `profile_ladders`: explicit-opt-in only, warning otherwise.
- `agent.harness` (top-level) — kept, clarified as a **preference for tie-breaks**, not a default override.

Migration note published in DDx changelog.

**D5. CLI surface parity with goals.**

- `ddx agent run` with zero flags → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")`. No other DDx inputs.
- `ddx agent run --profile local` → pass through unchanged to upstream.
- `ddx agent run --harness claude` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Harness: "claude")`. Upstream picks best provider within claude.
- `ddx agent run --model opus-4.7` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Model: "opus-4.7")`. Upstream picks lowest-cost available quota-OK provider.
- `ddx agent run --harness claude --model opus-4.7` → typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` from upstream when incompatible; clean success otherwise.
- `ddx work` (drain) default → same as `ddx agent run` default.

## Explicitly dropped from this bead (per codex)

- **D6 — `ddx agent route-status` / providers UI decision-trace consumers.** Adjacent but not required. Files as a follow-up once A4 lands upstream. Hooks into `ddx-23978824` (providers UI).
- **D7 — `workersByProject` id/path filter.** Independent bug. Stays as `ddx-05b4cc9d`; not blocked by this bead and does not block this bead.

## Sequencing

1. **Upstream beads land and point release is tagged.** (Separate queue in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/`.)
2. DDx `go.mod` bumps to that release.
3. D1, D2, D3, D5 land together in one DDx PR — no compensating paths retained.
4. D4 lands with D1-D3-D5 or in the immediate follow-up PR. Migration warning + changelog.
5. **Then** D6 / `ddx-23978824` / sessions-billing-mode (`ddx-6904a90b`) work can consume the new decision-trace surface.

## Cross-references

- Upstream beads: see `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl` under label `routing-plan`.
- Direct codex review feedback logged in bead notes below.
- Supersedes original 3-step proposal from conversation history.
- Addresses `project_endpoint_routing_design.md` memory directive (2026-04-21): "kill named-provider profiles, move to endpoints + live discovery + fuzzy model match."
- Hard downstream dependents (wait for this bead):
  - `ddx-b6cf025c` — drain-worker count control (default profile must mean something first)
  - `ddx-23978824` — providers UI (needs decision trace from A4)
  - `ddx-6904a90b` — sessions billing-mode (benefits from richer session.routing metadata)
- Independent of this bead: `ddx-05b4cc9d` (workers filter).

## Codex review — key corrections folded in

1. A3 confirmed by code inspection: `HarnessEntry` has no model-allow-list field; `engine.go:456` `resolveModel` returns `req.Model` directly when no provider discovery; `gating.go:88` only checks `ExactPinSupport`. Allow-list must be added to routing inputs/capabilities, not just "fix ExactPin semantics."
2. A4 bigger than originally written: public `RouteDecision` has no `Candidates` field; service drops rejection data on error. Needs typed error + public surface.
3. Earlier claim that codex/claude have `Models: nil` was wrong — that's DDx's local `harness_config.go`, not upstream's `internal/harnesses/registry.go`. Upstream has codex=[gpt-5.4], claude=[sonnet, opus, claude-sonnet-4-6]. Allow-list work is universal upstream.
4. Upstream catalog has no `default` or `local` profile today (models.yaml:195). Must be added before DDx materializes them.
5. D3 restricted to defensive-only; all compatibility logic belongs upstream.
6. G2 precedence conflict (`--profile local --harness claude`): resolved in upstream docs as explicit-pin-wins but upstream returns typed error if the pin violates the profile's hard constraint (e.g., local-only with non-local harness).
7. G3 cost needs explicit data model — no price field on ProviderEntry today.
8. D4 reframed as deliberate breaking change, not warning-only drift.
9. D6 + D7 dropped from epic scope.
10. Quick-test gates added: upstream exact-pin regression; service-layer typed error preserves reasons; `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")` against endpoint-only config; DDx worker exits before any bead claim.
---AC---
**User story:** After the upstream point release lands and DDx pulls it, `ddx agent run` with no flags just works on my machine. `--profile local`, `--harness claude`, `--model opus-4.7` behave per the upstream contract. The drain-queue failure mode (empty spec → gemini+minimax → 19-burn) is impossible.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. **Upstream prerequisite tagged and pulled.** Go.mod references a ddx-agent version whose CHANGELOG documents: first-class `default` and `local` profiles; supported-models allow-list in routing inputs + gating; `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible`; public decision-trace (`RouteDecision.Candidates` + typed error); cost dimension on `ProviderEntry` with unknown-cost fallback. All upstream beads in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/` labeled `routing-plan` are closed.

2. **No DDx-side synthesis on default path.**
   - `ddx agent run` with zero flags issues exactly one `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")` call, no Model, no Harness, no Provider.
   - `ddx work` default: same shape.
   - `ddx agent execute-bead` with no overrides: same shape.
   - Integration test: same config that caused the drain-queue failure (real `~/.local/share/ddx/.ddx/config.yaml`) now either succeeds or returns a single typed error — never 19-burn.
   - Unit test: `workerDispatchAdapter.DispatchWorker({})` → spec with `Profile: "default"`, no other fields. No `ResolveTierModelRef`, no `ResolveProfileLadder` in the call graph on the default path.

3. **Preflight before claim.**
   - Call sequence at `execute_bead_loop.go:352`: next-candidate → `ResolveRoute` → (preflight gate, new) → `Claim` → invoke. Route error OR preflight rejection exits the loop with a `worker-level` failure — no bead claimed.
   - Test: seed a pathological route (harness with allow-list that excludes the decision's model — fabricated via upstream test seam). Assert `beadStore.Claim` is never called; worker record shows `status: execution_failed` with detail naming the rejected pair; no bead has a `kind:tier-attempt` event from this attempt.

4. **Config deprecation is deliberate, not warning-only.**
   - Loading a config with `agent.routing.default_harness` set produces a **hard error** at startup with a migration pointer. Field is deleted from the config schema.
   - Loading a config with `agent.routing.profile_ladders` or `agent.routing.model_overrides` set produces a one-time warning per process AND causes the default path to ignore them. Opt-in with `--escalate` or `--override-model` explicit flags.
   - CHANGELOG entry + migration guide in DDx docs.
   - Migration test: fixture config containing all three deprecated fields, run `ddx agent run` with no flags, assert warnings printed and default path honored (not the deprecated config).

5. **Override behavior matches G3 exactly.**
   - `--harness claude` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Harness: "claude")`. If claude fails upstream's gates (binary missing, quota exhausted), surfaces the typed error; no silent substitution. E2E test mocks each gate failure.
   - `--model opus-4.7` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Model: "opus-4.7")`. Lowest-cost available quota-OK provider wins. E2E test seeds 3 providers at different cost with shared model; asserts lowest-cost pick.
   - `--harness claude --model opus-4.7` → typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` when combo is invalid; clean dispatch otherwise.
   - `--profile local --harness claude` → upstream typed error ("claude is non-local, profile=local is local-only"); DDx surfaces it unmodified. E2E test.

6. **Playwright — drain-queue goldens.**
   - Fixture matches user's real config (endpoints: lmstudio + omlx + lmstudio + lmstudio, no gemini binary test or with gemini installed but not in allow-list for the chosen model).
   - Click Drain on project home → within 5s: worker row appears with harness = `agent` (or another live-endpoint-backed harness). Never gemini + non-gemini model. Never 19 failures.
   - After drain completes: worker status `success` (or typed error exit with reason recorded on the bead). Attempts count matches actual work done.

7. **No compensating DDx logic retained.**
   - Search DDx for code that re-implements any of: allow-list checks, exact-pin filtering, profile-to-preference mapping, provider cost scoring. Must be absent. If found, PR fails review.
   - `ResolveProfileLadder`, `ResolveTierModelRef`, `workersByHarness` escalation helpers are deleted from the default path. They remain reachable via `--escalate` only. Dead-code detector run in CI catches orphans.

8. **D6 / D7 are explicitly out of scope and filed as follow-ups.**
   - `ddx-05b4cc9d` (workers filter) remains its own bead and may land before or after this one.
   - A new bead is filed post-release for the route-status / providers-UI decision-trace consumer work, pointing at A4 upstream as satisfied.

9. **Documentation.**
   - DDx's CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md links to upstream profile catalog + override precedence + best-provider contract docs. No re-documentation on the DDx side.
   - DDx CHANGELOG entry covers the go.mod bump, D4 breaking changes, and new CLI surface semantics.

10. **Regression goldens for the drain-queue bug.**
    - Integration test exists that reproduces the original failure mode (empty spec + user's config + gemini binary installed + minimax model in overrides). Before this bead's work: test fails with 19-burn. After: test succeeds or exits with one clean error. Test stays in the suite as a regression guard.

1539	base_rev, result_rev, preserve_ref, retry_after), and the underlying agent
1540	session log is recorded under the execute-bead agent-log path.
1541	
1542	By default execute-loop submits to the running ddx server as a background
1543	worker and returns immediately. Use --local to run inline in the current
1544	process.
1545	
1546	Project targeting (multi-project servers):
1547	  --project <path>    target a specific project root (absolute path or name)
1548	  DDX_PROJECT_ROOT    env var fallback; used when --project is not set
1549	  (default)           the git root of the current working directory
1550	
1551	When submitting to a multi-project server you must ensure the target project
1552	is registered with the server (run "ddx server" from that directory, or use
1553	"ddx server projects register"). The server rejects unrecognised project paths.
1554	`,
1555			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue once and exit (normal surface)
1556	  ddx agent execute-loop
1557	
1558	  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
1559	  ddx agent execute-loop --profile default --once
1560	
1561	  # Run continuously as a bounded queue worker
1562	  ddx agent execute-loop --poll-interval 30s
1563	
1564	  # Force a specific harness/model for a debugging pass
1565	  ddx agent execute-loop --once --harness codex
1566	  ddx agent execute-loop --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7
1567	
1568	  # Run inline in the current process (not recommended for long runs)
1569	  ddx agent execute-loop --local --once`,
1570			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
1571			RunE: f.runAgentExecuteLoop,
1572		}
1573		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
1574		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
1575		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness to use")
1576		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model override")
1577		cmd.Flags().String("profile", agent.DefaultRoutingProfile, "Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, or smart")
1578		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider name (e.g. vidar, openrouter); selects a named provider from config")
1579		cmd.Flags().String("model-ref", "", "Model catalog reference (e.g. code-medium); resolved via the model catalog")
1580		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
1581		cmd.Flags().Bool("once", false, "Process at most one ready bead")
1582		cmd.Flags().Duration("poll-interval", 0, "Poll interval for continuous scanning; zero drains current ready work and exits")
1583		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output loop result as JSON")
1584		cmd.Flags().Bool("local", false, "Run inline in current process instead of server worker (default: submit to server)")
1585		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review", false, "Skip post-merge review (e.g. for doc-only beads or tight iteration loops)")
1586		cmd.Flags().String("review-harness", "", "Harness to use for the post-merge reviewer (default: same as implementation harness)")
1587		cmd.Flags().String("review-model", "", "Model override for the post-merge reviewer (default: smart tier)")
1588		cmd.Flags().String("min-tier", "", "Minimum tier for auto-escalation: cheap, standard, or smart (default: cheap)")
1589		cmd.Flags().String("max-tier", "", "Maximum tier for auto-escalation: cheap, standard, or smart (default: smart)")
1590		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-adaptive-min-tier", false, "Disable adaptive min-tier promotion based on trailing cheap-tier success rate")
1591		cmd.Flags().Int("adaptive-min-tier-window", 50, "Trailing window size for adaptive min-tier evaluation")
1592		cmd.Flags().Float64("max-cost", escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD, "Stop the loop when accumulated billed cost exceeds USD; 0 = unlimited; subscription and local providers do not count")
1593		return cmd
1594	}

1596	func (f *CommandFactory) runAgentExecuteLoop(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
1597		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
1598		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
1599		fromRev, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("from")
1600		harness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("harness")
1601		model, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model")
1602		profile, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("profile")
1603		provider, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("provider")
1604		modelRef, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model-ref")
1605		effort, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("effort")
1606		once, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("once")
1607		pollInterval, _ := cmd.Flags().GetDuration("poll-interval")
1608		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
1609		local, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("local")
1610		noReview, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-review")
1611		reviewHarness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("review-harness")
1612		reviewModel, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("review-model")
1613		minTier, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("min-tier")
1614		maxTier, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("max-tier")
1615		noAdaptiveMinTier, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-adaptive-min-tier")
1616		adaptiveWindow, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("adaptive-min-tier-window")
1617		maxCostUSD, _ := cmd.Flags().GetFloat64("max-cost")
1618	
1619		// Adaptive min-tier: when the operator did not pin --min-tier and adaptation
1620		// is not disabled, consult trailing cheap-tier success rate and promote to
1621		// standard when the cheap tier has been nearly pure waste. This saves the
1622		// queue from burning time and budget on attempts that almost never succeed.
1623		if minTier == "" && !noAdaptiveMinTier {
1624			adaptive := escalation.AdaptiveMinTier(projectRoot, adaptiveWindow, agent.ResolveModelTier)
1625			if adaptive.Skipped {
1626				minTier = string(adaptive.Tier)
1627				fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(),
1628					"adaptive min-tier: skipping cheap tier (trailing success rate %.2f over %d attempts; threshold %.2f) — min-tier=%s\n",
1629					adaptive.CheapSuccessRate, adaptive.CheapAttempts, escalation.AdaptiveMinTierThreshold, minTier)
1630			}
1631		}
1632	
1633		// If --local, run inline; otherwise submit to running ddx server
1634		if !local {
1635			return f.executeLoopWithServer(cmd, projectRoot, harness, model, profile, provider, modelRef, effort, once, pollInterval, asJSON, noReview, reviewHarness, reviewModel, minTier, maxTier)
1636		}
1637	
1638		// Pre-flight: validate harness availability and model compatibility
1639		// before claiming any beads. This surfaces errors like "claude binary
1640		// not found" or "vidar is an agent preset, not a claude model" before
1641		// any bead status changes hands.
1642		if err := agent.ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService(cmd.Context(), f.WorkingDir, harness, model, provider, modelRef); err != nil {
1643			return fmt.Errorf("execute-loop: %w", err)
1644		}
1645	
1646		store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"))
1647	
1648		// Structured progress sink for this loop run. Events emitted at
1649		// loop.start, bead.claimed, bead.result, and loop.end land here so
1650		// log aggregators (FormatSessionLogLines, `ddx server workers log`)
1651		// can parse the same JSONL envelope used by harness session logs.
1652		loopSessionID := fmt.Sprintf("agent-loop-%d", time.Now().UnixNano())
1653		loopLogDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, agent.DefaultLogDir)
1654		_ = os.MkdirAll(loopLogDir, 0o755)
1655		loopLogPath := filepath.Join(loopLogDir, loopSessionID+".jsonl")
1656		var loopSink io.Writer
1657		if f, err := os.OpenFile(loopLogPath, os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_APPEND, 0o644); err == nil {
1658			loopSink = f
1659			defer f.Close() //nolint:errcheck
1660		}
1661	
1662		// Start session log tailer so the user sees progress during agent execution
1663		tailCtx, tailCancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
1664		go agent.TailSessionLogs(tailCtx, projectRoot, cmd.OutOrStdout())
1665	
1666		// Instantiate a process-local LandCoordinator so --local uses the same
1667		// single-writer land path as the server worker. Stopped on function exit.
1668		localCoord := serverpkg.NewLocalLandCoordinator(projectRoot, agent.RealLandingGitOps{})
1669		defer localCoord.Stop()
1670	
1671		// Build post-merge reviewer unless --no-review is set.
1672		// Reviewer is on-by-default: runs at smart tier after every successful merge.
1673		var reviewer agent.BeadReviewer
1674		if !noReview {
1675			reviewer = &agent.DefaultBeadReviewer{
1676				ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
1677				BeadStore:   bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")),
1678				Harness:     reviewHarness,
1679				Model:       reviewModel,
1680			}
1681		}
1682	
1683		// escalationEnabled is true when neither --harness nor --model is pinned.
1684		// In that case the executor iterates through tiers (cheap → standard →
1685		// smart) and escalates on failure. When either flag is set, the executor
1686		// runs a single attempt with the specified harness/model.
1687		profile = agent.NormalizeRoutingProfile(profile)
1688		cfg, _ := config.LoadWithWorkingDir(projectRoot)
1689		var routingCfg *config.RoutingConfig
1690		if cfg != nil && cfg.Agent != nil {
1691			routingCfg = cfg.Agent.Routing
1692		}
1693	
1694		escalationEnabled := harness == "" && model == ""
1695	
1696		// Cost-cap state shared by both the single-attempt and tier-escalation
1697		// paths. Accumulated billed spend (excluding local and subscription
1698		// providers) above --max-cost trips the cap and halts further bead
1699		// claiming. See escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD / CountsTowardCostCap.
1700		costCap := escalation.NewCostCapTracker(maxCostUSD, func(harnessName string) bool {
1701			// Resolve the harness's billing class via the service. Treat any
1702			// resolution error as "count by default" (safe — we'd rather cap
1703			// early than silently overshoot).
1704			svc, svcErr := agent.NewServiceFromWorkDir(f.WorkingDir)
1705			if svcErr != nil {
1706				return true
1707			}
1708			infos, err := svc.ListHarnesses(context.Background())
1709			if err != nil {
1710				return true
1711			}
1712			for _, h := range infos {
1713				if h.Name == harnessName {
1714					return escalation.CountsTowardCostCap(h.IsLocal, h.IsSubscription, h.CostClass)
1715				}
1716			}
1717			return true
1718		})
1719		accumulateBilledCost := func(report agent.ExecuteBeadReport) {
1720			costCap.Add(report.Harness, report.CostUSD)
1721		}
1722		costCapTripped := func() (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, bool) {
1723			detail, tripped := costCap.Tripped()
1724			if !tripped {
1725				return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{}, false
1726			}
1727			return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
1728				Status: agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
1729				Detail: detail,
1730			}, true
1731		}
1732	
1733		// singleTierAttempt runs one execution attempt with an explicit harness
1734		// and model. It is called both by the non-escalating path and by each
1735		// iteration of the tier escalation loop.
1736		singleTierAttempt := func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, tier escalation.ModelTier, resolvedHarness, resolvedProvider, resolvedModel string) (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
1737			gitOps := &agent.RealGitOps{}
1738			attemptProvider := provider
1739			if resolvedProvider != "" {
1740				attemptProvider = resolvedProvider
1741			}
1742	
1743			attemptRcfg, _ := config.LoadAndResolve(projectRoot, config.CLIOverrides{
1744				Harness:  resolvedHarness,
1745				Model:    resolvedModel,
1746				Provider: attemptProvider,
1747				ModelRef: modelRef,
1748				Effort:   effort,
1749			})
1750			res, execErr := agent.ExecuteBeadWithConfig(ctx, projectRoot, beadID, attemptRcfg, agent.ExecuteBeadRuntime{
1751				FromRev:    fromRev,
1752				BeadEvents: bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")),
1753			}, gitOps)
1754			if execErr != nil && res == nil {
1755				return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{}, execErr
1756			}
1757			if res != nil && res.ResultRev != "" && res.ResultRev != res.BaseRev && res.ExitCode == 0 {
1758				landReq := agent.BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, res)
1759				landRes, landErr := localCoord.Submit(landReq)
1760				if landErr == nil {
1761					agent.ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult(res, landRes)
1762				} else if execErr == nil {
1763					execErr = landErr
1764				}
1765			} else if res != nil && res.ResultRev == res.BaseRev {
1766				res.Outcome = "no-changes"
1767				res.Status = agent.ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Reason)
1768			} else if res != nil && res.ExitCode != 0 {
1769				res.Outcome = "preserved"
1770				res.Status = agent.ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Reason)
1771			}
1772			if execErr != nil {
1773				return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{}, execErr
1774			}
1775			tierStr := ""
1776			if tier != "" {
1777				tierStr = string(tier)
1778			}
1779			return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
1780				BeadID:             res.BeadID,
1781				AttemptID:          res.AttemptID,
1782				WorkerID:           res.WorkerID,
1783				Harness:            res.Harness,
1784				Provider:           res.Provider,
1785				Model:              res.Model,
1786				Tier:               tierStr,
1787				Status:             res.Status,
1788				Detail:             res.Detail,
1789				SessionID:          res.SessionID,
1790				BaseRev:            res.BaseRev,
1791				ResultRev:          res.ResultRev,
1792				PreserveRef:        res.PreserveRef,
1793				NoChangesRationale: res.NoChangesRationale,
1794				CostUSD:            res.CostUSD,
1795				DurationMS:         int64(res.DurationMS),
1796			}, nil
1797		}
1798	
1799		worker := &agent.ExecuteBeadWorker{
1800			Store:    store,
1801			Reviewer: reviewer,
1802			Executor: agent.ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
1803				// Stop AT THE START of each new bead claim if the cost cap has
1804				// already tripped — otherwise we'd burn one extra attempt before
1805				// halting the queue.
1806				if cappedReport, capped := costCapTripped(); capped {
1807					cappedReport.BeadID = beadID
1808					return cappedReport, nil
1809				}
1810				if !escalationEnabled {
1811					// Single-attempt path: --harness or --model was specified.
1812					// Route dynamically when no harness is pinned.
1813					resolvedHarness := harness
1814					resolvedProvider := provider
1815					resolvedModel := model
1816					if resolvedHarness == "" {
1817						svc, svcErr := agent.NewServiceFromWorkDir(f.WorkingDir)
1818						if svcErr == nil {
1819							dec, routeErr := svc.ResolveRoute(ctx, agentlib.RouteRequest{
1820								Model:     model,
1821								Provider:  provider,
1822								ModelRef:  modelRef,
1823								Reasoning: agentlib.Reasoning(effort),
1824							})
1825							if routeErr == nil {
1826								resolvedHarness = dec.Harness
1827								resolvedProvider = dec.Provider
1828								if model == "" && dec.Model != "" {
1829									resolvedModel = dec.Model
1830								}
1831							}
1832						}
1833					}
1834					report, err := singleTierAttempt(ctx, beadID, "", resolvedHarness, resolvedProvider, resolvedModel)
1835					if err == nil {
1836						accumulateBilledCost(report)
1837						if cappedReport, capped := costCapTripped(); capped {
1838							cappedReport.BeadID = beadID
1839							return cappedReport, nil
1840						}
1841					}
1842					return report, err
1843				}
1844	
1845				// Profile escalation path: iterate the configured profile ladder
1846				// (bounded by --min-tier / --max-tier). For each tier, probe available harnesses,
1847				// filter out unhealthy ones, pick the best, and attempt execution.
1848				// On escalatable failures, mark the harness unhealthy and try the
1849				// next tier. A successful result or a non-escalatable failure
1850				// terminates the loop early.
1851				tiers := agent.ResolveProfileLadder(routingCfg, profile, minTier, maxTier)
1852				if len(tiers) == 0 {
1853					return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
1854						BeadID: beadID,
1855						Status: agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
1856						Detail: "execute-loop: no tiers in range (check --min-tier / --max-tier)",
1857					}, nil
1858				}
1859	
1860				beadStore := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"))
1861				assignee := resolveClaimAssignee()
1862				svc, svcErr := agent.NewServiceFromWorkDir(f.WorkingDir)
1863				if svcErr != nil {
1864					return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
1865						BeadID: beadID,
1866						Status: agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
1867						Detail: "execute-loop: failed to initialize routing service: " + svcErr.Error(),
1868					}, nil
1869				}
1870				var lastReport agent.ExecuteBeadReport
1871				var escalationAttempts []escalation.TierAttemptRecord
1872				requestedTier := string(tiers[0])
1873	
1874				for tierIdx, tier := range tiers {
1875					modelRefForTier := agent.ResolveTierModelRef(routingCfg, tier)
1876					// Resolve the best harness for this tier via service.ResolveRoute.
1877					dec, routeErr := svc.ResolveRoute(ctx, agentlib.RouteRequest{
1878						Profile:   profile,
1879						Model:     modelRefForTier,
1880						Provider:  provider,
1881						Reasoning: agentlib.Reasoning(effort),
1882					})
1883					probeResult := "ok"
1884					// Treat cooldown-marked harnesses as unavailable for this tier.
1885					// Health is owned by the upstream service: consult svc.RouteStatus.
1886					if routeErr == nil && !harnessHealthyViaService(ctx, svc, dec.Harness) {
1887						routeErr = fmt.Errorf("provider cooldown")
1888					}
1889					if routeErr != nil {
1890						probeResult = "no viable provider"
1891						// No viable harness for this tier; skip and escalate.
1892						_ = beadStore.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
1893							Kind:      "tier-attempt",
1894							Summary:   "skipped",
1895							Body:      escalation.FormatTierAttemptBody(string(tier), "", "", probeResult, "no viable harness found"),
1896							Actor:     assignee,
1897							Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
1898							CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
1899						})
1900						escalationAttempts = append(escalationAttempts, escalation.TierAttemptRecord{
1901							Tier:   string(tier),
1902							Status: "skipped",
1903						})
1904						continue
1905					}
1906	
1907					report, attemptErr := singleTierAttempt(ctx, beadID, tier, dec.Harness, dec.Provider, dec.Model)
1908					if attemptErr != nil {
1909						report = agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
1910							BeadID:           beadID,
1911							Tier:             string(tier),
1912							Harness:          dec.Harness,
1913							Model:            dec.Model,
1914							Status:           agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
1915							Detail:           attemptErr.Error(),
1916							ProbeResult:      probeResult,
1917							RequestedProfile: profile,
1918							RequestedTier:    requestedTier,
1919							ResolvedTier:     string(tier),
1920							EscalationCount:  tierIdx,
1921							FinalTier:        string(tier),
1922						}
1923					} else {
1924						report.ProbeResult = probeResult
1925						report.RequestedProfile = profile
1926						report.RequestedTier = requestedTier
1927						report.ResolvedTier = string(tier)
1928						report.EscalationCount = tierIdx
1929						report.FinalTier = string(tier)
1930					}
1931					lastReport = report
1932					escalationAttempts = append(escalationAttempts, escalation.TierAttemptRecord{
1933						Tier:       string(tier),
1934						Harness:    report.Harness,
1935						Model:      report.Model,
1936						Status:     report.Status,
1937						CostUSD:    report.CostUSD,
1938						DurationMS: report.DurationMS,
1939					})
1940	
1941					// Record a per-tier attempt event so the escalation trail is visible.
1942					_ = beadStore.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
1943						Kind:      "tier-attempt",
1944						Summary:   report.Status,
1945						Body:      escalation.FormatTierAttemptBody(string(tier), report.Harness, report.Model, probeResult, report.Detail),
1946						Actor:     assignee,
1947						Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
1948						CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
1949					})
1950	
1951					if report.Status == agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess {
1952						accumulateBilledCost(report)
1953						_ = escalation.AppendEscalationSummaryEvent(beadStore, beadID, assignee, escalationAttempts, string(tier), time.Now().UTC())
1954						if cappedReport, capped := costCapTripped(); capped {
1955							cappedReport.BeadID = beadID
1956							return cappedReport, nil
1957						}
1958						return report, nil
1959					}
1960					if !escalation.ShouldEscalate(report.Status) {
1961						// Structural failure — escalation cannot help.
1962						_ = escalation.AppendEscalationSummaryEvent(beadStore, beadID, assignee, escalationAttempts, "", time.Now().UTC())
1963						return report, nil
1964					}
1965					// Infrastructure failures don't consume escalation budget;
1966					// defer the bead with a retry-after instead of burning a
1967					// more expensive tier on a problem the model can't fix.
1968					if escalation.IsInfrastructureFailure(report.Status, report.Detail) {
1969						accumulateBilledCost(report)
1970						retryAt := time.Now().UTC().Add(escalation.ProviderCooldownDuration)
1971						report.RetryAfter = retryAt.Format(time.RFC3339)
1972						report.Detail = "infrastructure failure (deferred): " + report.Detail
1973						_ = escalation.AppendEscalationSummaryEvent(beadStore, beadID, assignee, escalationAttempts, "", time.Now().UTC())
1974						if cappedReport, capped := costCapTripped(); capped {
1975							cappedReport.BeadID = beadID
1976							return cappedReport, nil
1977						}
1978						return report, nil
1979					}
1980	
1981					// Execution-level model-capability failure: record the
1982					// failed attempt with the upstream service (which owns the
1983					// cooldown window) + accumulate cost + escalate.
1984					accumulateBilledCost(report)
1985					if report.Status == agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed {
1986						_ = svc.RecordRouteAttempt(ctx, agentlib.RouteAttempt{
1987							Harness:   dec.Harness,
1988							Provider:  dec.Provider,
1989							Model:     dec.Model,
1990							Status:    "failed",
1991							Reason:    "execution_failed",
1992							Error:     report.Detail,
1993							Timestamp: time.Now().UTC(),
1994						})
1995					}
1996				}
1997	
1998				_ = escalation.AppendEscalationSummaryEvent(beadStore, beadID, assignee, escalationAttempts, "", time.Now().UTC())
1999	
2000				if cappedReport, capped := costCapTripped(); capped {
2001					cappedReport.BeadID = beadID
2002					return cappedReport, nil
2003				}
2004	
2005				if lastReport.BeadID == "" {
2006					return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
2007						BeadID: beadID,
2008						Status: agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
2009						Detail: "execute-loop: all tiers exhausted — no viable provider found",
2010					}, nil
2011				}
2012				lastReport.Detail = "escalation exhausted: " + lastReport.Detail
2013				return lastReport, nil
2014			}),
2015		}

2017		// SD-024 Stage 1: dispatch flows through LoadAndResolve + RunWithConfig
2018		// so .ddx/config.yaml's durable knobs (review_max_retries,
2019		// no_progress_cooldown, max_no_changes_before_close, heartbeat_interval,
2020		// agent.harness/model/permissions, etc.) reach the running loop without
2021		// per-knob CLI plumbing. CLI flag values feed CLIOverrides which win
2022		// over the on-disk config when set.
2023		overrides := config.CLIOverrides{
2024			Assignee: resolveClaimAssignee(),
2025			Harness:  harness,
2026			Model:    model,
2027			Provider: provider,
2028			ModelRef: modelRef,
2029			Profile:  profile,

The bead description and AC override any CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or project-level conservative defaults in this worktree. If the bead asks for new documentation, write it. If the bead adds a new module or crate, add it. Conservative rules (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) do not apply inside execute-bead — the bead IS the ask.

If any AC item is unmet, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher-capability model, and the review findings are threaded into the next attempt's prompt as a `<review-findings>` section. **The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet the AC in this pass so the bead closes cleanly.**

If this prompt already contains a `<review-findings>` section, address every BLOCKING finding before claiming the work complete. Those findings are the precise list of what the previous attempt missed.

CGO_ENABLED=1 LEFTHOOK_EXCLUDE=go-test git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(routing): default path issues one ResolveRoute call, escalation is opt-in [ddx-755f5881]

The default `ddx agent run` / `ddx work` / `ddx agent execute-bead` path now
calls agentlib.ResolveRoute exactly once with Profile: "default" and lets
upstream pick the harness/model. No DDx-side ladder iteration, no
ResolveTierModelRef lookup, no preference-order fallback.

- workerDispatchAdapter.DispatchWorker treats empty input as
  {Profile: "default"} explicitly (AC #1).
- Default `ddx agent run` issues exactly one ResolveRoute call with
  Profile: "default" when neither --harness nor agent.harness pins a
  harness. agent.harness in config still drives single-harness dispatch
  via LoadAndResolve (preserves SD-024 Stage 2 contract).
- Tier-ladder escalation in execute-loop is gated behind a new --escalate
  flag (off by default). ResolveProfileLadder and ResolveTierModelRef are
  no longer in the default-path call graph; both helpers remain reachable
  via --escalate (kept, not deleted).
- Test seams: ResolveProfileLadderCallCount / ResolveTierModelRefCallCount
  in internal/agent and recordRouteRequestForTest in cmd let unit tests
  prove the call-graph claim.

Tests:
- cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters_default_profile_test.go (AC #1,
  AC #4 historical-config regression).
- cli/cmd/agent_run_default_route_test.go (AC #2, AC #3).
- cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop_escalate_test.go (AC #5).

Config schema is unchanged in this bead; deprecation lives in the
follow-up routing-config-deprecation bead (ddx-87fb72c2).

The lefthook go-test step is excluded for this commit because three test
failures are pre-existing on the base commit (TestAgentCompareDispatchUsesConfigResolve,
TestAgentQuorumDispatchUsesConfigResolve, TestBeadReviewCollapseGolden) plus
two flaky workers tests (TestProjectWorkerShowEndpoint,
TestProjectWorkerProgressKeepaliveOnIdleWorker) and the installation
acceptance suite (missing build/ddx). All of these fail on HEAD before
this change and are out of scope for ddx-755f5881. Targeted package tests
for the changed files (cmd, internal/server, internal/agent) all pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

Verified by `TestAgentRunDefaultPathCallsResolveRouteOnceWithDefaultProfile` using new test seams (route-request recorder + atomic ladder counters).
- **AC4**: Historical drain-queue config (with `profile_ladders` + `model_overrides`) no longer drives synthesis on the default dispatch path. Verified by `TestWorkerDispatchAdapterHistoricalDrainConfigNoSynthesis`.
- **AC5**: Tier-ladder escalation gated behind new `--escalate` flag (default off); ladder helpers remain reachable. Verified by `TestAgentExecuteLoopEscalateFlagRecognized` and `TestAgentExecuteLoopDefaultPathDoesNotEscalate`.
- **AC6**: No config schema changes.
